message:
feat(dashboard/panels): adopt shared state components and add alert pulsing

Migrate dashboard panels to use the new shared RenderState components
for consistent empty/loading/error states, and add visual pulsing for
newly-appeared alerts to draw attention.

AlertsPanel changes (alerts.go):
- Add firstSeen map to track when each alert was first observed
- Add alertKey() for stable alert identification (uses ID or fallback)
- Add shouldPulse() returning true for alerts < 3 seconds old
- New alerts pulse with styles.Pulse() for visual attention
- Use bounded tick (% 10_000) to prevent int overflow on 32-bit platforms
- Inject time.Now for testability via now field

BeadsPanel changes (beads.go):
- Replace inline error styling with components.ErrorState()
- Add loading state when Available=false and Reason is empty
- Add error state when Available=false and Reason is populated
- Both states include "Press r to refresh" remediation hint

CASSPanel changes (cass.go):
- Replace inline error styling with components.ErrorState()
- Replace inline empty styling with components.EmptyState()
- Consistent width handling (w-4 for panel padding)

Test coverage added:
- TestAlertsPanelTracksFirstSeenByID: verifies stable tracking
- TestAlertsPanelPrunesFirstSeenWhenAlertsRemoved: verifies cleanup
- TestAlertsPanelAlertKeyFallbackWhenIDMissing: verifies fallback key
- TestBeadsPanelViewUnavailableWithReason: verifies reason display

Design rationale:
- Shared components ensure visual consistency across all panels
- Alert pulsing provides temporal awareness without being disruptive
- Pulsing respects NTM_REDUCE_MOTION (via styles.Pulse)
- Time injection enables deterministic testing

Part of Premium UX Polish (ntm-952) and Performance (ntm-952.6) epics.
Closes ntm-953 (alerts pulse tick overflow fix).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
